questions.question: Let A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {a, b, c}. Find the Cartesian product A x B and describe a relation R on A x B such that {(1, a), (2, b), (3, c)} ∈ R.
questions.hint: This relation R should be reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

questions.question: Prove that the Cartesian product A x B is commutative for any sets A and B.
questions.hint: You may use the definition of the Cartesian product.

questions.question: Show that the relation R = {(x, y) ∈ A x B | x = y} on A x B is an equivalence relation.
questions.hint: To prove R is an equivalence relation, show it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
